# Adversary Candidate: Three-outcome (c) vs. handoff-trigger contradiction exploit

**Exploit 1 (Primary)**

Input scenario: student receives a trivial one-word change request from a STEERING.md that exists and is readable. After applying the change, the student has no iteration-3 teacher-approval signal to calibrate confidence.

The three-outcome rule's outcome (c) fires when "approval still looks unlikely after one refinement" — an inferential state. But the teacher-handoff trigger says "only trigger it on an observable workspace condition." These two rules are irresolvably contradictory for any scenario where outcome (c) legitimately applies.

**Predicted score vs. original:** 0.32 vs. 0.67

**Patch applied:** Added "or when the three-outcome self-check reaches outcome (c)" and "when the active-iteration STEERING.md exists but contains no revision instructions" as explicit observable conditions in the teacher-handoff trigger paragraph. This makes outcome (c) itself an observable state that authorizes the escalation.
